channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ontario Court Strikes Down Air Travel Complaint Confidentiality Clause
item.description: An Ontario Superior Court judge has ruled that regulations preventing travellers from sharing outcomes of passenger complaints with Canada’s transport regulator violate Canadians’ Charter rights to fr...

item.title: Insurers urged to build trust and improve education to boost insurance uptake in Tanzania
item.description: The insurance industry in Tanzania has been called upon to prioritize building public trust, enhancing customer education, and developing products that address real risks to increase insurance uptake....

item.title: Thousands gather in Mashhad as Iran buries Ali Khamenei amid renewed regional tensions
item.description: Thousands of mourners gathered in Mashhad on Thursday for the burial of Iran's assassinated Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, bringing to a close six days of funeral ceremonies held across Iran and Iraq. T...

item.title: New York Yankees' Anthony Volpe Defends Team-First Mentality Amid Position Change Rumors
item.description: Anthony Volpe, the New York Yankees' shortstop, addressed rumors about a potential position change after a retracted report claimed he was considering moving to catcher. In an interview, Volpe dismiss...

item.title: Karolina Muchova advances to Wimbledon final after thrilling tiebreak victory
item.description: Karolina Muchova, the 10th seed, secured her place in her first Wimbledon final by defeating Coco Gauff in a dramatic deciding tiebreak. The match, which ended 6-1, 2-6, 7-6 (12-10), saw Muchova save ...

item.title: Australia and India agree to uranium exports for nuclear energy collaboration
item.description: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi finalized a landmark agreement to export Australian uranium to India for use in its nuclear energy sector. The deal, ...

item.title: Ontario Man Discharged After Being Deemed No Longer a Risk to Public Safety
item.description: A Toronto man with a history of severe sexual and violent offenses has been granted an absolute discharge by the Ontario Review Board (ORB), concluding he no longer poses a significant risk to public ...

item.title: Civil society urges stronger human rights reforms as Tanzania prepares for fourth UN UPR review
item.description: Tanzania is continuing preparations for its fourth Universal Periodic Review (UPR), which is expected to take place in Geneva between October and November this year. As part of these preparations, the...

item.title: House of Representatives Demands Stronger Diplomatic Measures Against Xenophobic Attacks in South Africa
item.description: The Nigerian House of Representatives has called on the Federal Government to take strong diplomatic action against South Africa over renewed xenophobic attacks, extrajudicial killings, and systematic...

item.title: Tanzania pushes for safer digital spaces to empower women and combat gender-based violence
item.description: Dr Dorothy Gwajima, Tanzania’s Minister for Community Development, Gender, Women and Special Groups, called for safer digital spaces to empower women and combat gender-based violence (GBV) during the ...

item.title: PM Shehbaz reaffirms commitment to combat India-backed terrorism in Balochistan
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif addressed a high-level security meeting in Quetta, reaffirming Pakistan's resolve to combat India-backed terrorism in Balochistan. The PM highlighted recent terrorist att...

item.title: Senate Summons Zuleika Lopez for Sara Duterte Impeachment Trial
item.description: The Senate impeachment court has summoned Zuleika Lopez, the chief of staff of Vice President Sara Duterte, to appear in her impeachment trial. Lopez was ordered to attend hearings on July 13 and 14, ...

item.title: Tanzanian Government Commends Citizens for Maintaining Peace and Stability
item.description: Gerson Msigwa, Tanzania's Chief Spokesperson, praised Tanzanians for rejecting disruptive activities on July 7, highlighting their commitment to national stability. During his visit to the Dar es Sala...

item.title: Isro confirms cryogenic engine readiness for LVM-3 mission
item.description: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has successfully completed the flight acceptance hot test for the indigenous CE20 cryogenic engine, which will power the upper stage of the next LVM3 laun...

item.title: Kenya's Parliament Challenges High Court Over Cabinet Gender Composition
item.description: Parliament in Kenya has decided to appeal a High Court ruling that declared President William Ruto's Cabinet unconstitutional due to failing the two-thirds gender rule. The court found the Cabinet, wi...

item.title: Geo News Issues Apology for Muharram Documentary, Faces Criticism
item.description: Geo News has faced widespread praise from political, social, and media figures for its formal apology regarding the 'Safar-e-Ishq' documentary aired on 10th Muharram. The broadcaster acknowledged its ...

item.title: Bonnie Tyler, iconic singer of 'Total Eclipse of the Heart,' passes away at 75
item.description: Bonnie Tyler, the British singer famous for her power ballad 'Total Eclipse of the Heart,' has died at age 75 after a prolonged illness. Her family confirmed her death in a statement shared on Instagr...

item.title: Former KDF Soldier Shares Harrowing Experience Fighting in Russian Military
item.description: Former Kenya Defence Forces (KDF) soldier Dishon Adam, who joined the Russian army in 2023, has shared a detailed account of his experiences fighting in the Russian military. After serving 10 years in...

item.title: Ebola outbreak in DRC is fastest-growing, kills 600 people
item.description: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) is escalating rapidly, with health authorities confirming 1,759 cases and 600 deaths since mid-May 2026. This marks the fastest-growing out...

item.title: England's Key Players Miss Training Amid Injuries and Suspensions Ahead of Norway Clash
item.description: Declan Rice, Marc Guehi, and Reece James have opted for individual training sessions instead of joining the rest of the England World Cup squad, as the team prepares for their quarterfinal match again...
